question: What is the motive force for natural circulation in NuScale?

nuscale_ch01_0056.title: Critical Heat Flux Testing - Preliminary Fuel Design
nuscale_ch01_0056.summary: This section outlines the verification and confirmation testing programs for unique NuScale Power Module design features, focusing on areas lacking prior data or operational experience. Specifically, it describes critical heat flux (CHF) testing for the preliminary fuel design, which differs from standard PWR fuel in assembly height and coolant flow, and provided essential data for safety analysis and the development of NuScale's CHF correlation.

nuscale_ch01_0018.title: Principal Design Criteria
nuscale_ch01_0018.summary: The section outlines the principal design criteria for the NuScale plant, focusing on the Site Cooling Water System (SCWS), which supplies cooling water to auxiliary systems and is tailored to specific site requirements. It also describes the reactor's heat transfer process, natural circulation flow, and the generation of electricity via a steam turbine, with provisions for heat removal through a turbine bypass and condenser system.

nuscale_ch01_0044.title: Chemical and Volume Control System
nuscale_ch01_0044.summary: The Chemical and Volume Control System section describes the NuScale reactor’s passive decay heat removal system, which uses two independent trains, each capable of removing 100% of the decay heat via natural circulation and passive condensers immersed in the reactor pool. In case of a steam generator tube failure or loss of AC power, the system isolates the affected components and continues cooling the reactor by transferring heat to a large, stainless steel-lined ultimate heat sink pool, ensuring safe heat removal even during accidents.

nuscale_ch01_0064.title: NuScale Test and Inspection Plans
nuscale_ch01_0064.summary: This section describes NuScale’s test and inspection plans for key reactor components, focusing on the control rod drive shafts and the Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS) valves. It summarizes successful tests confirming operability, drop time, and impact force of control rods, as well as a dedicated test program for ECCS valves—including their unique features and performance under reactor conditions—conducted at a specialized facility to ensure safety and functionality.

nuscale_ch01_0063.title: Emergency Core Cooling System Valve Design Certification Application Demonstration Test
nuscale_ch01_0063.summary: This section describes the demonstration test program for the Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS) valves in the NuScale reactor design. The program aimed to verify the functional performance of the ECCS valves, including their operation at reactor pressures and temperatures and in borated coolant, as well as the effectiveness of the inadvertent actuation block (IAB) feature. Testing was conducted at a specialized facility using a test article representative of the reactor recirculation valve (RRV) to ensure the valves meet safety and design requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0019.title: Operating Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0019.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) operates using natural circulation for reactor coolant flow, eliminating the need for reactor coolant pumps, and features a small, modular, integral pressurized water reactor design. It relies on passive safety systems—including dedicated emergency core cooling and decay heat removal systems—that do not require electrical power, and its components are partially immersed in a reactor pool for enhanced safety. The NPM allows for flexible power maneuvering using control rods and soluble boron, and its design minimizes external piping and penetrations below the reactor core.

nuscale_ch01_0055.title: NuScale Testing Programs
nuscale_ch01_0055.summary: This section outlines the NuScale testing programs designed to verify and confirm unique design features supporting the plant’s safety analysis, particularly where prior data or operational experience was lacking. It summarizes completed and ongoing tests for the NuScale Power Module, including those related to fuel design, steam generator, control rod assemblies, and integrated system phenomena, with specific mention of critical heat flux testing for both preliminary and final fuel designs to support licensing and safety analysis.

nuscale_ch01_0054.title: Requirements for Additional Technical Information
nuscale_ch01_0054.summary: This section outlines the verification and confirmation testing programs for unique design features of the NuScale Power Plant, which provide essential data to support the final safety analyses. It details completed and ongoing tests focused on areas lacking prior data or operational experience, including specific tests for fuel design, steam generators, control rod assemblies, and integrated system phenomena. The section also describes critical heat flux (CHF) testing conducted for both preliminary and final fuel designs to meet licensing requirements and support safety analysis.

nuscale_ch01_0059.title: Steam Generator Thermal-Hydraulic Performance Testing - Fluid-Heated Facility
nuscale_ch01_0059.summary: The section describes thermal-hydraulic performance testing of the NuScale steam generator using an electrically-heated, fluid-heated facility at SIET in Italy. The tests, which included various operational scenarios, allowed precise control and measurement of heat flux and pressure dynamics to support design validation, code benchmarking, and regulatory inspection, as well as to inform component sizing and operational stability.

nuscale_ch01_0058.title: Steam Generator Thermal-Hydraulic Performance Testing - Electrically Heated Facility
nuscale_ch01_0058.summary: This section describes the critical heat flux (CHF) testing conducted for the NuScale fuel design using an electrically heated 5x5 simulated fuel bundle with AREVA HTP/HMP spacer grid technology. The tests aimed to gather CHF data, validate and develop CHF correlations, and measure thermal-hydraulic characteristics under various operating conditions, supporting the safety analysis for the NuScale Power Module. Testing was performed at the AREVA Karlstein Thermal Hydraulics facility in Germany.

nuscale_ch01_0811.title: DSRS 15.9.A, Rev 0: Thermal Hydraulic Stability Review ResponsibilitiesII.5 Detect and Suppress Method Trip of reactor before SAFDL violation
nuscale_ch01_0811.summary: The NuScale design does not use the exclusion zone or detect-and-suppress methods for thermal hydraulic stability; instead, existing reactor trip signals act preemptively to prevent violation of safety limits before flow instabilities can develop. The system partially conforms to regulatory criteria by ensuring reactor trips occur before Safety Analysis Fuel Design Limits (SAFDL) are exceeded, but does not actively detect or suppress instabilities. Backup options and additional monitoring systems are not applicable, as the current trip system is considered sufficient.
